channel.title: CyberNetSec - Threat Actor
channel.description: Latest threat actor threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Pro-Iranian Group 'Handala' Claims 'Warning' Attack on California Water Systems
item.description:
The pro-Iranian hacktivist group 'Handala' has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against California water infrastructure. The group framed the intrusion as a retaliatory 'warning' to the U.S. for alleged strikes on Iranian water resources. While Handala asserted it did not disrupt water suppl...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Conti Ransomware Coder Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court for Wire Fraud Conspiracy
item.description:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko, a 44-year-old Ukrainian national, has pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court to conspiracy to commit wire fraud. He admitted to his role in the notorious Conti ransomware gang, which was responsible for over $150 million in ransom payments. Lytvynenko, who was arrest...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack dubbed 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR). Attackers claim ownership of these abandoned packages and modify their build scripts (PKGBUILD) to fetch and install a malicious npm pa...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Researcher Drops 'GreatXML' Zero-Day Exploit to Bypass Windows BitLocker
item.description:
A security researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse has publicly released details of 'GreatXML,' a new unpatched zero-day exploit that allegedly bypasses Windows BitLocker encryption. The exploit allows an attacker with physical access to a machine to gain SYSTEM-level privileges in the Windows Recovery ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Oracle Rushes Emergency Patch for PeopleSoft Zero-Day Exploited by ShinyHunters
item.description:
Oracle has released an emergency, out-of-band patch for a critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability in its PeopleSoft PeopleTools software, tracked as CVE-2026-35273. The urgent release follows confirmation that the flaw is being actively exploited in the wild by th...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Global Schools Foundation Suffers Major Data Breach, Exposing 33,000 Passports
item.description:
The Global Schools Foundation (GSF), an international K-12 education organization, has been impacted by a significant data breach attributed to the threat actor 'FulcrumSec'. The incident, reported on June 11, 2026, resulted in the exposure of highly sensitive personal information of both students a...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Deploys Self-Propagating Malware in Global RaaS Campaign
item.description:
Microsoft has uncovered a highly sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation named 'The Gentlemen,' tracked as Storm-2697. This financially motivated group has developed a Go-based ransomware that features robust encryption and, most notably, an aggressive self-propagation module designed...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Vietnam's OceanLotus APT Pivots to Domestic Spying, Hits Construction and Finance Sectors
item.description:
The Vietnam-aligned threat group OceanLotus (also known as APT32) has shifted its focus to domestic espionage, targeting a Vietnamese construction firm and stock market investors. According to research from ESET, the group conducted a year-long intrusion and a sophisticated supply-chain attack compr...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Supply Chain Attack, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Russian APTs Persistently Exploit Year-Old WinRAR Flaw in Attacks on Ukraine
item.description:
A high-severity vulnerability in WinRAR, CVE-2025-8088, patched nearly a year ago, is still being actively exploited by multiple Russian-linked APT groups against targets in Ukraine. According to Trend Micro, the Gamaredon (UAC-0010) and SHADOW-EARTH-066 (UAC-0226) groups are using the flaw to deliv...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Energy Sector in Crosshairs: 66% of APT Campaigns Target Utilities, Report Finds
item.description:
A new intelligence report from CYFIRMA reveals a concentrated and sustained cyber espionage campaign against the global energy and utilities sector. Over the past three months, this critical infrastructure sector was the target in 66% of all observed Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) campaigns. The m...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →
